instruction:
Let $X(t) = \xi \cos(\beta t + \eta)$, where $\beta$ is a constant and the random variable $\xi$ follows a Rayleigh distribution $(\sigma > 0)$
$$f_\xi(x) = \begin{cases} \dfrac{x}{\sigma^2} \mathrm{e}^{-\frac{x^2}{2\sigma^2}}, & x > 0 \\ \\ 0, & x \leq 0 \end{cases}$$
Additionally, $\eta \sim U(0, 2\pi)$, and $\xi$ and $\eta$ are independent of each other. Find the mean function $m_{X}(t)$ and the covariance function $C_{X}(s, t)$ of the stochastic process $\left\{X(t), -\infty < t < +\infty\right\}$ is ().

Options:
A. $$
m(t)=\sigma
$$
$$
\begin{array} {l} {{{{{C ( s, t )=\beta\cdot\operatorname{c o s} ( \sigma( s+t ) )}}}}} \\ \end{array}
$$
B. $$
m(t)=0
$$
$$
\begin{array} {l} {{{{{C ( s, t )=\sigma\cdot\operatorname{s i n} ( \beta(s+t) )}}}}} \\ \end{array}
$$
C. $$
m(t)=0
$$
$$
\begin{array} {l} {{{{{C ( s, t )=\beta^{2} \cdot\operatorname{s i n} ( \beta( s-t ) )}}}}} \\ \end{array}
$$
D. $$
m(t)=0
$$
$$
\begin{array} {l} {{{{{C ( s, t )=\sigma^{2} \cdot\operatorname{c o s} ( \beta( s-t ) )}}}}} \\ \end{array}
$$
E. $$
m(t)=\sigma
$$
$$
\begin{array} {l} {{{{{C ( s, t )=\pi\cdot\operatorname{c o s} ( \beta s t ) )}}}}} \\ \end{array}
$$
F. $$
m(t)=\pi
$$
$$
\begin{array} {l} {{{{{C ( s, t )=\sigma^{2}\cdot\operatorname{s i n} ( \beta( s+t ) )}}}}} \\ \end{array}
$$
G. $$
m(t)=\pi
$$
$$
\begin{array} {l} {{{{{C ( s, t )=\beta\cdot\operatorname{c o s} ( \sigma s t ) )}}}}} \\ \end{array}
$$
H. $$
m(t)=\sigma
$$
$$
\begin{array} {l} {{{{{C ( s, t )=\sigma\cdot\operatorname{s i n} ( \beta( s+t ) )}}}}} \\ \end{array}
$$
I. $$
m(t)=\pi
$$
$$
\begin{array} {l} {{{{{C ( s, t )=\beta\cdot\operatorname{c o s} ( \sigma( s-t ) )}}}}} \\ \end{array}
$$
J. $$
m(t)=0
$$
$$
\begin{array} {l} {{{{{C ( s, t )=\beta^{2} \cdot\operatorname{c o s} ( \sigma( s-t ) )}}}}} \\ \end{array}
$$
